title: Rate problems 2
topic_path: Module 1: Relationships between quantities and reasoning with equations and their graphs
Topic D: Creating equations to solve problems

assessment_items.question_content:
Starting at home, Ishaan traveled uphill to the grocery store for $12$ minutes at just $15$ mph. He then traveled back home along the same path downhill at a speed of $30$ mph. 

 **What is his average speed for the entire trip from home to the grocery store and back?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] mph
hints: The average speed is not just the average of $15$ mph and $30$ mph.
He traveled for a longer time uphill (since he was going slower), so we can estimate that the average speed is closer to $15$ mph than $30$ mph.

To calculate the average speed, we will make use of the following:

 $ \text{average speed} = \dfrac{\blue{\text{total distance}}}{\pink{\text{total time}}} $

 $\text{distance uphill} = \text{distance downhill}$
What was the total distance traveled?

$ \blue{\begin{align*} \text{total distance} &= \text{distance uphill} + \text{distance downhill} \\ &= 2 \times \text{distance uphill} \end{align*}} $

 $ \begin{align*} \text{distance uphill} &= \text{speed uphill} \times \text{time uphill} \\ &= 15\text{ mph} \times 12\text{ minutes} \times \dfrac{1 \text{ hour}}{60 \text{ minutes}}\\ &= 3\text{ miles} \end{align*} $

Substituting to find the total distance:

 $\blue{\text{total distance} = 6\text{ miles}}$
What was the total time spent traveling?

$ \pink{\text{total time} = \text{time uphill} + \text{time downhill}} $

 $ \begin{align*} \text{time downhill} &= \dfrac{\text{distance downhill}}{\text{speed downhill}} \\ &= \dfrac{3\text{ miles}}{30\text{ mph}} \times \dfrac{60 \text{ minutes}}{1 \text{ hour}}\\ &= 6\text{ minutes} \end{align*} $
$ \pink{\begin{align*} \text{total time} &= 12\text{ minutes} + 6\text{ minutes} \\ &= 18\text{ minutes} \end{align*}} $
Now that we know both the total distance and total time, we can find the average speed.

$ \begin{align*} \text{average speed} &= \dfrac{\blue{\text{total distance}}}{\pink{\text{total time}}} \\ &= \dfrac{\blue{6\text{ miles}}} {\pink{18\text{ minutes}}} \times \dfrac{60 \text{ minutes}}{1 \text{ hour}} \\ &= 20\text{ mph} \end{align*} $
The average speed is $20$ mph, and which is closer to $15$ mph than $30$ mph as we expected.

assessment_items.question_content:
Starting at home, Jessica traveled uphill to the toy store for $12$ minutes at just $10$ mph. She then traveled back home along the same path downhill at a speed of $30$ mph. 

 **What is her average speed for the entire trip from home to the toy store and back?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] mph
hints: The average speed is not just the average of $10$ mph and $30$ mph.
She traveled for a longer time uphill (since she was going slower), so we can estimate that the average speed is closer to $10$ mph than $30$ mph.

To calculate the average speed, we will make use of the following:

 $ \text{average speed} = \dfrac{\blue{\text{total distance}}}{\pink{\text{total time}}} $

 $\text{distance uphill} = \text{distance downhill}$
What was the total distance traveled?

$ \blue{\begin{align*} \text{total distance} &= \text{distance uphill} + \text{distance downhill} \\ &= 2 \times \text{distance uphill} \end{align*}} $

 $ \begin{align*} \text{distance uphill} &= \text{speed uphill} \times \text{time uphill} \\ &= 10\text{ mph} \times 12\text{ minutes} \times \dfrac{1 \text{ hour}}{60 \text{ minutes}}\\ &= 2\text{ miles} \end{align*} $

Substituting to find the total distance:

 $\blue{\text{total distance} = 4\text{ miles}}$
What was the total time spent traveling?

$ \pink{\text{total time} = \text{time uphill} + \text{time downhill}} $

 $ \begin{align*} \text{time downhill} &= \dfrac{\text{distance downhill}}{\text{speed downhill}} \\ &= \dfrac{2\text{ miles}}{30\text{ mph}} \times \dfrac{60 \text{ minutes}}{1 \text{ hour}}\\ &= 4\text{ minutes} \end{align*} $
$ \pink{\begin{align*} \text{total time} &= 12\text{ minutes} + 4\text{ minutes} \\ &= 16\text{ minutes} \end{align*}} $
Now that we know both the total distance and total time, we can find the average speed.

$ \begin{align*} \text{average speed} &= \dfrac{\blue{\text{total distance}}}{\pink{\text{total time}}} \\ &= \dfrac{\blue{4\text{ miles}}} {\pink{16\text{ minutes}}} \times \dfrac{60 \text{ minutes}}{1 \text{ hour}} \\ &= 15\text{ mph} \end{align*} $
The average speed is $15$ mph, and which is closer to $10$ mph than $30$ mph as we expected.

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] mph
hints: The average speed is not just the average of $6$ mph and $12$ mph.

To calculate the average speed, we will make use of the following:

 $ \text{average speed} = \dfrac{\blue{\text{total distance}}}{\pink{\text{total time}}} $

 $\text{distance uphill} = \text{distance downhill}$
What was the total distance traveled?

$ \blue{\begin{align*} \text{total distance} &= \text{distance uphill} + \text{distance downhill} \\ &= 2 \times \text{distance uphill} \end{align*}} $

 $ \begin{align*} \text{distance uphill} &= \text{speed uphill} \times \text{time uphill} \\ &= 6\text{ mph} \times 60\text{ minutes} \times \dfrac{1 \text{ hour}}{60 \text{ minutes}}\\ &= 6\text{ miles} \end{align*} $

Substituting to find the total distance:

 $\blue{\text{total distance} = 12\text{ miles}}$
What was the total time spent traveling?

$ \pink{\text{total time} = \text{time uphill} + \text{time downhill}} $

 $ \begin{align*} \text{time downhill} &= \dfrac{\text{distance downhill}}{\text{speed downhill}} \\ &= \dfrac{6\text{ miles}}{12\text{ mph}} \times \dfrac{60 \text{ minutes}}{1 \text{ hour}}\\ &= 30\text{ minutes} \end{align*} $
$ \pink{\begin{align*} \text{total time} &= 60\text{ minutes} + 30\text{ minutes} \\ &= 90\text{ minutes} \end{align*}} $
Now that we know both the total distance and total time, we can find the average speed.

$ \begin{align*} \text{average speed} &= \dfrac{\blue{\text{total distance}}}{\pink{\text{total time}}} \\ &= \dfrac{\blue{12\text{ miles}}} {\pink{90\text{ minutes}}} \times \dfrac{60 \text{ minutes}}{1 \text{ hour}} \\ &= 8\text{ mph} \end{align*} $
The average speed is $8$ mph, and which is closer to $6$ mph than $12$ mph as we expected.

assessment_items.question_content:

What was the total distance traveled?

$ \blue{\begin{align*} \text{total distance} &= \text{distance uphill} + \text{distance downhill} \\ &= 2 \times \text{distance uphill} \end{align*}} $

Substituting to find the total distance:

 $\blue{\text{total distance} = 4\text{ miles}}$
What was the total time spent traveling?

$ \pink{\text{total time} = \text{time uphill} + \text{time downhill}} $

 $ \begin{align*} \text{time downhill} &= \dfrac{\text{distance downhill}}{\text{speed downhill}} \\ &= \dfrac{2\text{ miles}}{12\text{ mph}} \times \dfrac{60 \text{ minutes}}{1 \text{ hour}}\\ &= 10\text{ minutes} \end{align*} $
$ \pink{\begin{align*} \text{total time} &= 30\text{ minutes} + 10\text{ minutes} \\ &= 40\text{ minutes} \end{align*}} $
Now that we know both the total distance and total time, we can find the average speed.

$ \begin{align*} \text{average speed} &= \dfrac{\blue{\text{total distance}}}{\pink{\text{total time}}} \\ &= \dfrac{\blue{4\text{ miles}}} {\pink{40\text{ minutes}}} \times \dfrac{60 \text{ minutes}}{1 \text{ hour}} \\ &= 6\text{ mph} \end{align*} $
The average speed is $6$ mph, and which is closer to $4$ mph than $12$ mph as we expected.

hints: The average speed is not just the average of $8$ mph and $24$ mph.

What was the total distance traveled?

 $ \begin{align*} \text{distance uphill} &= \text{speed uphill} \times \text{time uphill} \\ &= 8\text{ mph} \times 30\text{ minutes} \times \dfrac{1 \text{ hour}}{60 \text{ minutes}}\\ &= 4\text{ miles} \end{align*} $

 $ \begin{align*} \text{time downhill} &= \dfrac{\text{distance downhill}}{\text{speed downhill}} \\ &= \dfrac{4\text{ miles}}{24\text{ mph}} \times \dfrac{60 \text{ minutes}}{1 \text{ hour}}\\ &= 10\text{ minutes} \end{align*} $

The average speed is $12$ mph, and which is closer to $8$ mph than $24$ mph as we expected.

assessment_items.question_content:
Starting at home, Luis traveled uphill to the gift store for $50$ minutes at just $6$ mph. He then traveled back home along the same path downhill at a speed of $12$ mph. 

 **What is his average speed for the entire trip from home to the gift store and back?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] mph
hints: The average speed is not just the average of $6$ mph and $12$ mph.
He traveled for a longer time uphill (since he was going slower), so we can estimate that the average speed is closer to $6$ mph than $12$ mph.

To calculate the average speed, we will make use of the following:

 $ \text{average speed} = \dfrac{\blue{\text{total distance}}}{\pink{\text{total time}}} $

 $\text{distance uphill} = \text{distance downhill}$
What was the total distance traveled?

$ \blue{\begin{align*} \text{total distance} &= \text{distance uphill} + \text{distance downhill} \\ &= 2 \times \text{distance uphill} \end{align*}} $

 $ \begin{align*} \text{distance uphill} &= \text{speed uphill} \times \text{time uphill} \\ &= 6\text{ mph} \times 50\text{ minutes} \times \dfrac{1 \text{ hour}}{60 \text{ minutes}}\\ &= 5\text{ miles} \end{align*} $

Substituting to find the total distance:

 $\blue{\text{total distance} = 10\text{ miles}}$
What was the total time spent traveling?

$ \pink{\text{total time} = \text{time uphill} + \text{time downhill}} $

 $ \begin{align*} \text{time downhill} &= \dfrac{\text{distance downhill}}{\text{speed downhill}} \\ &= \dfrac{5\text{ miles}}{12\text{ mph}} \times \dfrac{60 \text{ minutes}}{1 \text{ hour}}\\ &= 25\text{ minutes} \end{align*} $
$ \pink{\begin{align*} \text{total time} &= 50\text{ minutes} + 25\text{ minutes} \\ &= 75\text{ minutes} \end{align*}} $
Now that we know both the total distance and total time, we can find the average speed.

$ \begin{align*} \text{average speed} &= \dfrac{\blue{\text{total distance}}}{\pink{\text{total time}}} \\ &= \dfrac{\blue{10\text{ miles}}} {\pink{75\text{ minutes}}} \times \dfrac{60 \text{ minutes}}{1 \text{ hour}} \\ &= 8\text{ mph} \end{align*} $
The average speed is $8$ mph, and which is closer to $6$ mph than $12$ mph as we expected.

assessment_items.question_content:
It takes $20$ minutes for $5$ people to paint $5$ walls. 

 ** How many minutes does it take $9$ people to paint $9$ walls? **

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] minutes
hints: Imagine that each person is assigned one wall, and all $5$ people begin painting at the same time.
Since everyone will finish painting their assigned wall after $20$ minutes, it takes one person $20$ minutes to paint one wall.
If we have $9$ people and $9$ walls, we can again assign one wall to each person.
Everyone will take $20$ minutes to paint their assigned wall.
In other words, it takes $20$ minutes for $9$ people to paint $9$ walls.
